opp.name: Centrient Pharmaceuticals_S2P
opp.next_step: BrainBox (Jai Bharat Maram) scouting the market and shortlisting vendors on Centrient's behalf, with Zycus to be positioned within that scan. Demo to be arranged with Kristin Boehme, and pricing approach to be finalised. ERP migration timing gives the window to align the Ariba replacement.
opp.ais_why: They want to get rid of SAP Ariba, due to lack of user adoption, capabilities to the price they are paying. Want to have something like Merlin Intake in their system and currently have asked their partner team to scout the market for the same.

items.quote: Zero buyer calls, emails, or activity on record. No discovery run to uncover business problem, pain, quantified value case, or evaluation criteria.
items.source: Avoma discovery 26 Jun 2026 (opp/account/attendee pulls), SF LastActivityDate null, SF Task/Event queries

items.quote: All MEDDPICC elements are gaps: no Metrics, no Economic Buyer, no Decision Criteria, no Decision Process, no Paper Process, no identified Pain, no Champion, no competitive intelligence beyond incumbent Ariba.
items.source: SF Q2/Q3 synthesis fields empty, zero buyer calls

items.quote: Single buyer-side contact role (Kristin Boehme, Director Global Procurement). No multi-threading to Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, supply chain leadership, IT, or finance. Single point of contact risk is high.

No competitors named beyond incumbent Ariba. BrainBox scouting market and shortlisting vendors, but shortlist unknown to Zycus. No visibility into who else is being evaluated or buyer priorities for differentiation.
items.source: SF Competitors__c empty, Next_Step__c 2 Jun 2026, zero buyer calls

items.area: ERP migration timing and forcing function
items.quote: Next Step references ERP migration timing as window to align Ariba replacement, but no dates, no migration plan details, no confirmed forcing function or urgency.

BrainBox consultant Jai Bharat Maram positioning Zycus in vendor scan, but unclear whether BrainBox is advisory only or implementation SI, and whether contract is direct with Centrient or partner-channeled. Commercial structure ambiguous.
items.source: SF Next_Step__c 2 Jun 2026, OpportunityContactRole (Jai Bharat Maram, BrainBox, Evaluator)

metrics.narrative: No business problem, pain, or quantified value case documented anywhere. Salesforce fields Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, and Gaps_identified_during_sales_demo__c are all empty. Zero buyer calls on record means no qualitative or quantitative pain articulated by the prospect. The Next Step references an ERP migration timing window to align Ariba replacement, implying procurement stack modernization, but this is unconfirmed and carries no business case, cost savings target, cycle-time improvement, or operational pain stated by the relevant stakeholder discovery has been run to uncover the problem or quantify the impact. The single highest-leverage move is to run a discovery call with Kristin Boehme (Director Global Procurement) and/or the BrainBox consultant to extract the business problem, current pain with Ariba or legacy systems, and quantify the value case (cost, headcount, cycle time, error rate, savings/ROI target).

champion.narrative: No champion identified. Salesforce field X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c is empty. No Contact Role of Champion exists. Zero buyer calls means no evidence of advocacy, internal selling, or access to power. The single buyer-side contact role is Kristin Boehme (Director Global Procurement, Evaluator), who may be a potential champion candidate given her procurement leadership role, but no call or engagement proves she is advocating for Zycus, has introduced us to the EB or DM, shared internal evaluation docs, or pushed back on competitors on our behalf. Her engagement level is unknown (passive vs active). The Next Step implies BrainBox consultant Jai Bharat Maram is positioning Zycus within a vendor scan, which suggests BrainBox may be an advocate, but a third-party consultant is not a champion (they do not own the business problem or have power within Centrient's org). The deal is single-threaded to Kristin via BrainBox with no direct Zycus-Centrient relationship established. Developing a champion within Centrient procurement or supply chain leadership is a top-3 priority. The move is to establish direct contact with Kristin Boehme, assess her engagement and advocacy potential, and ask for introductions to the broader buying committee (supply chain head, finance, IT) to multi-thread and identify a true champion.

competition.narrative: No competitors documented. Salesforce fields Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c are empty. Zero buyer calls means no competitive shortlist named by Centrient or BrainBox. The Next Step states BrainBox is scouting the market and shortlisting vendors on Centrient's behalf, which implies a multi-vendor evaluation is starting or planned, but the field is unknown. The Next Step also references Ariba replacement, confirming SAP Ariba is the incumbent being displaced, but no other rivals (Coupa, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua, Zycus competitors) are named. Without knowing the competitive set, Zycus cannot tailor differentiation, positioning, or the demo/RFP response. The move is to ask BrainBox and Kristin Boehme during the first call: who else is being evaluated, what is the shortlist, why is Ariba being replaced (price, fit, lack of AI, poor UX), and what differentiation matters most to the relevant stakeholder the competitive landscape is foundational for positioning Merlin AI and the full S2P stack wedge.

identify_pain.narrative: No pain identified or articulated by the buyer. Salesforce fields Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c are empty. Zero buyer calls means no operational or financial pain stated by the relevant stakeholder Step references ERP migration timing as a window to align Ariba replacement, which implies dissatisfaction with the current Ariba stack or a forcing function to modernize procurement systems during the ERP cutover, but this is inferred, not confirmed. The pain owner is unknown (could be procurement, IT, finance, or supply chain leadership). No specifics on what is broken (user adoption, lack of AI/automation, poor supplier collaboration, invoice cycle time, maverick spend, integration complexity) or the cost of the status quo. Without confirmed pain, Zycus cannot build urgency or tailor the value story. The move is to run a discovery call with Kristin Boehme and/or Nikolaas Rossillion (if involved) to uncover the specific pain with the current stack, the ERP migration timeline and implications, and the consequences of inaction (the 'what if this is not done' forcing function).

paper_process.narrative: No paper process or contracting mechanics documented. Salesforce fields referencing legal, InfoSec, MSA/SOW, procurement channel, or partner involvement are empty. Zero buyer calls means no discussion of contract vehicle, legal review, InfoSec questionnaire, or implementation scoping. The Next Step references pricing approach to be finalized, but no clarity on whether this is a direct contract with Centrient or channeled through BrainBox, and whether BrainBox is acting as implementation SI or simply a vendor-selection consultant. No Current_Contract_Expiration__c date exists to anchor a forcing function (e.g. Ariba renewal deadline). The deal is in Qualified stage, so contracting is distant, but the partner involvement (BrainBox) creates ambiguity: if BrainBox is the buyer (reseller model), Centrient may never be the contracting party; if BrainBox is advisory only, Centrient contracts directly. Clarifying the commercial structure and BrainBox's role is necessary before advancing to the relevant stakeholder or Negotiation. The move is to confirm with Caroline Lacocque and BrainBox whether this is a direct Centrient contract or partner-channeled deal, and surface any known legal/InfoSec gates early.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified. Salesforce fields X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c are empty. No Contact Role of Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor exists. Zero buyer calls means no commercial or pricing conversation has occurred to reveal who controls budget authority. The account-level contact list includes a CFO (Ronald Merckx) and finance directors (Shalabh Goel, Ugur Sayan, Gertjan Feenstra, Oscar Montero), but none are confirmed stakeholders in this S2P opportunity, and no call or email ties them to this deal. The buyer-side contact role is Kristin Boehme (Director Global Procurement, Evaluator role), who is likely an influencer or decision-maker for vendor selection but not the budget owner for a $250k, 9-product S2P stack. The Next Step implies BrainBox is running vendor shortlisting on Centrient's behalf, which may indicate budget authority sits with procurement leadership (possibly Nikolaas Rossillion, Global Head of Supply Chain, Procurement, on the account contact list) or finance, but this is unconfirmed. Identifying and mapping the EB is a top-3 priority; the deal cannot advance to commercials or Vendor Selected stage without it.

decision_process.narrative: No decision process mapped. Salesforce fields What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Multiple_approvals__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c are empty. Zero buyer calls means no org-chart visibility, no approval chain documented, and no process steps (shortlist, demo, POC, proposal, EB sign-off, legal, InfoSec, contract) have been discussed. The single contact role (Kristin Boehme, Evaluator) and partner contact (Jai Bharat Maram, BrainBox consultant) suggest a partner-led vendor scouting process, but the buyer's internal decision steps, committee structure, and approval gates are unknown. The Next Step implies BrainBox is driving the shortlist on Centrient's behalf, which may indicate a consulting-led procurement process, but Zycus has no direct visibility into Centrient's buying committee, timeline, or decision authority. The deal cannot advance without mapping the process. The move is to ask BrainBox and Kristin Boehme to walk through the steps: who evaluates, who approves budget, who signs the contract, and what gates (demo, RFP response, pricing, legal, InfoSec) must be cleared before selection.

decision_criteria.narrative: No decision criteria documented. Salesforce fields Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c, Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c are empty. Zero buyer calls means no use-case reviews, test-case sessions, RFP/RFI rounds, or workshops have been run to surface evaluation criteria. The products scope (9 modules including Merlin AI) implies a full S2P evaluation, but no BRD, fit matrix, or stated priorities exist. The Next Step references BrainBox scouting the market and shortlisting vendors, which suggests an RFI or vendor-scan process may be starting, but criteria are not yet defined or shared with the relevant stakeholder deal sits in Qualified stage with no evidence of a structured evaluation process. Without criteria, Zycus cannot position differentiation, tailor demos, or validate fit. The move is to request the BRD or evaluation framework from BrainBox/Kristin Boehme during the first discovery call and confirm whether this is an RFI, RFP, or informal shortlist process.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

An advisor (BrainBox) is scouting the market on Centrient's behalf with a demo still to be arranged; no meetings on record

Products scope includes Merlin (AI agents), so AI is in the deal scope, but fit is unvalidated. Cannot tier as AI Hungry, AI Curious, or AI Resistant without discovery. Under-positioned until AI appetite is surfaced and Merlin story is delivered.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 7 May 2026 in Qualified stage. Last modified 2 Jun 2026 (24 days ago) when Next_Step__c was updated to reflect BrainBox partner-led vendor scouting and a planned demo with Kristin Boehme. No buyer engagement, calls, emails, or activity recorded since creation. No stage advancement. Zero forward momentum. The Next Step describes a future plan (BrainBox positioning Zycus, demo to be arranged, pricing to be finalized, ERP migration timing window) but no execution evidence. The deal is dark and has never truly launched.

items.detail: No Economic Buyer or Decision Maker identified. No executive-level engagement. Single point of contact (Director-level procurement, Kristin Boehme) via third-party consultant. No visibility into CFO, finance, or C-suite sponsorship for a $250k, 9-module S2P stack. EB mapping is a condition for advancing to the relevant stakeholder or Negotiation.

Single-threaded relationship through BrainBox consultant with no direct Zycus-Centrient connection. If BrainBox favors another vendor or the relationship cools, Zycus loses all access. No internal champion, no multi-threading to buying committee. High ghost risk.

ERP migration timing referenced as forcing function for Ariba replacement, but no migration dates, no urgency confirmed, no compelling event validated. Timeline ambiguity creates slip risk and may signal this is a future-state placeholder rather than an active 2027-Q1 deal.

Zero buyer engagement 50 days post-creation. No discovery, no demo, no MEDDPICC coverage, no competitive intel. Deal may be a placeholder or already lost in a vendor scan Zycus is not actively participating in. Dark deal risk is critical.

champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole 26 Jun 2026 (1 buyer contact, 1 partner contact), zero buyer calls (Avoma discovery 26 Jun 2026), SF Next_Step__c 2 Jun 2026
champion_strength.summary: No champion. Kristin Boehme (Director Global Procurement, Evaluator role) is the single buyer-side contact, but zero engagement evidence means her advocacy level, access to power, and willingness to champion Zycus are unknown. BrainBox consultant Jai Bharat Maram is positioning Zycus in the vendor scan per the Next Step, but a third-party consultant is not a champion (no internal authority, no ownership of the business problem). The deal is single-threaded through a partner with no direct Zycus-Centrient relationship. Champion development is a top-3 priority.

items.action: Contact BrainBox and Kristin Boehme this week to confirm Zycus inclusion in vendor scan and schedule discovery call.
items.trigger: Deal is 50 days old with zero buyer engagement. Next Step updated 2 Jun 2026 (24 days ago) describing BrainBox scouting market and positioning Zycus, but no execution evidence. Without contact this week, Zycus risks being dropped from shortlist or never added.
items.expected_effect: Re-establishes contact, confirms Zycus is in the vendor scan, surfaces competitive shortlist and buyer priorities, and books the discovery/demo call to launch MEDDPICC discovery. Prevents ghost risk.

items.action: Run full MEDDPICC discovery call with Kristin Boehme and BrainBox to uncover business problem, pain, quantified value case, competitive shortlist, evaluation criteria, decision process, and Economic Buyer identity.
items.trigger: All MEDDPICC elements are gaps. Zero buyer calls on record. No business problem, pain, or quantified value case documented. No competitive intelligence beyond incumbent Ariba. No Decision Criteria, no Decision Process, no EB. Deal cannot advance to Shortlisted or Vendor Selected without discovery.
items.expected_effect: Uncovers Metrics, Pain, Decision Criteria, competitive landscape, and EB identity. Enables Zycus to position differentiation, tailor demo, and validate fit. Moves deal from dark/placeholder to active evaluation with clear next steps.

items.action: Multi-thread beyond Kristin Boehme by requesting introductions to Economic Buyer, supply chain head, IT lead, and broader buying committee.
items.trigger: Single buyer-side contact role (Kristin Boehme, Director Global Procurement). No Economic Buyer, no Decision Maker, no supply chain, IT, or finance engagement. Single point of contact via third-party consultant creates ghost risk and blocks MEDDPICC discovery.
items.expected_effect: Establishes direct Zycus-Centrient relationships with power roles (EB, DM, supply chain/IT leadership). Reduces partner-dependency risk. Enables EB mapping, champion development, and multi-threaded selling. Condition for advancing past Qualified stage.

items.action: Confirm ERP migration timeline and how it creates urgency for Ariba replacement to establish compelling event.
items.trigger: Next Step references ERP migration timing as window to align Ariba replacement (2 Jun 2026), but no migration dates, no urgency validated, no compelling event confirmed. Timeline ambiguity creates slip risk.
items.expected_effect: Validates or debunks the ERP migration forcing function. If real, anchors the deal timeline and urgency. If future-state or unclear, resets close date expectations and surfaces whether this is an active 2027-Q1 deal or a placeholder.

items.action: Surface AI appetite and position Merlin negotiation and contracting agents as differentiation wedge during discovery call.
items.trigger: Products scope includes Merlin (AI agents for negotiation, contracting, intake), but no AIS score, no buyer call discussing AI priorities, and no Merlin positioning. Zero discovery means Zycus does not know if Centrient has AI/automation priorities or interest in Merlin. Under-positioned risk is high.
items.expected_effect: Surfaces AI appetite and RFP AI requirements. If buyer has AI priorities, positions Merlin negotiation/contracting agents and full S2P stack integration as modern, AI-native differentiation Ariba and competitors cannot match. Validates product scope fit and raises win probability if AI is a buyer priority.

High risk of being a placeholder, a future-state opportunity not yet active, or already lost to a competitor Zycus has no visibility into. The Next Step describes a plan but shows no execution 24 days later. Single-threaded through a partner consultant with no direct buyer contact. All MEDDPICC elements are gaps. No champion, no EB, no competitive intel, no discovery. Confidence is very low.

Stage Qualified with 278 days to close (2027-03-31) allows ample runway, but the deal has never moved past initial qualification and shows no signs of active buyer engagement. No time-in-stage submission dates available. No forward movement since creation.

north_star_verdict.headline: Zero buyer engagement in 8+ months since May 2026 creation. No calls, emails, or activity logged. Partner BrainBox promised scouting but never executed. Single-thread, no champion verified, no pain documented. Deal exists only as Salesforce entry with no proof of.

best_practice_check.summary: Dark deal with zero buyer engagement 50 days post-creation. Single-threaded through partner consultant with no direct Zycus-Centrient relationship. All MEDDPICC elements are gaps. No champion, no EB, no competitive intel, no discovery. High risk of being a placeholder or losing to a more engaged competitor in the BrainBox vendor scan.

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba confirmed as the incumbent being replaced per the Next Step field (2 Jun 2026), which references ERP migration timing as the window to align Ariba replacement. No other competitors named in any Salesforce field or call. The Next Step states BrainBox is scouting the market and shortlisting vendors on Centrient's behalf, which implies a multi-vendor evaluation is starting, but the shortlist is unknown and Zycus has no visibility into who else is being considered (Coupa, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua, or other S2P platforms). With zero buyer calls, there is no competitive intelligence on why Ariba is being displaced (cost, lack of AI/automation, poor fit, integration complexity, UX issues), no stated differentiation priorities, and no confirmation of Zycus's standing in the scan. The single strongest current threat is UNKNOWN COMPETITIVE FIELD, not Ariba (the incumbent is being replaced, so it is a `do_nothing` alternative at most, not a retention fight). How we win is also unknown until the shortlist, buyer priorities, and evaluation criteria are surfaced. The rank-1 move is to ask BrainBox and Kristin Boehme who else is being evaluated and why Ariba is out, then position Merlin AI negotiation/contracting agents and full S2P stack integration as the differentiation wedge if AI appetite and procurement automation are buyer priorities.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: ERP migration timing gives the window to align the Ariba replacement
competitors.how_we_win: Ariba is being replaced, so threat is low unless they re-engage with a retention offer. Win by positioning Merlin AI (negotiation, contracting, intake agents) and full S2P stack integration as the modern, AI-native alternative Ariba cannot match, especially if buyer priorities include procurement automation, supplier collaboration, and AI-driven savings. Must first confirm why Ariba is out (cost, fit, lack of innovation) and whether they are still in the running or fully eliminated.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning unknown. Products scope includes Merlin (AI agents for negotiation, contracting, intake, etc.) and iRisk, suggesting AI/automation is in scope, but no AIS score, no buyer call discussing AI appetite, and no validation of Merlin fit or priority. The Next Step does not mention AI. Zero discovery means Zycus does not know if Centrient has AI/automation priorities, RFP AI requirements, or interest in Merlin agents. Under-positioned risk is high given the comprehensive product scope (9 modules) and the lack of any AI value story delivered to the buyer. The move is to surface AI appetite during discovery and position Merlin negotiation/contracting agents as the differentiation wedge if procurement automation and AI-driven savings are buyer priorities.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Cannot assess fit. Zero buyer calls, no BRD, no stated evaluation criteria, no use-case discussions, no demo run. Products scope (9 modules including Merlin AI) implies Centrient needs a full S2P stack, but no validation of priorities, must-haves, or deal-breakers exists. The Next Step references demo to be arranged and pricing approach to be finalized, but these have not occurred. Fit is unconfirmed.

hard.ais_why: They want to get rid of SAP Ariba, due to lack of user adoption, capabilities to the price they are paying. Want to have something like Merlin Intake in their system and currently have asked their partner team to scout the market for the same.
hard.next_step: BrainBox (Jai Bharat Maram) scouting the market and shortlisting vendors on Centrient's behalf, with Zycus to be positioned within that scan. Demo to be arranged with Kristin Boehme, and pricing approach to be finalised. ERP migration timing gives the window to align the Ariba replacement.

deltas.subject: RE-ENGAGE IMMEDIATELY via BrainBox and Kristin Boehme. Deal is 50 days old with zero buyer interaction. The Next Step (updated 2 Jun, 24 days ago) describes a plan (BrainBox scouting, demo to be arranged, pricing to be finalized) but shows no execution. Without a discovery call or demo in the next 7 days, Zycus risks being dropped from the shortlist or never making it onto the list. The single highest-leverage move is for Caroline Lacocque (deal owner) to contact Jai Bharat Maram (BrainBox) and Kristin Boehme directly this week to confirm Zycus is still in the vendor scan, schedule the discovery/demo call, and surface the competitive shortlist, buyer priorities, and evaluation timeline. If no reply in 10 business days, escalate to John Woodcock (owner's manager) for exec-to-exec reach-out to Centrient procurement leadership (Nikolaas Rossillion, Global Head of Supply Chain, Procurement, if confirmed as stakeholder).

deltas.subject: RUN FULL DISCOVERY to uncover Metrics, Pain, Decision Criteria, and the competitive shortlist. Zero MEDDPICC coverage means Zycus cannot position differentiation, tailor the demo, or validate fit. Use the first buyer call (with Kristin Boehme and/or BrainBox) to extract: (1) the business problem and pain with current Ariba stack (cost, lack of AI/automation, poor UX, integration complexity, supplier collaboration gaps), (2) quantified value case or savings target, (3) the ERP migration timeline and how it creates urgency for Ariba replacement, (4) the competitive shortlist (who else is being evaluated), (5) evaluation criteria and BRD/must-haves, (6) the decision process and approval chain (who evaluates, who approves budget, who signs), (7) the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker identities. Without this discovery, the deal cannot advance to Shortlisted or Vendor Selected, and Zycus risks being eliminated in a process we are not actively shaping.

value.title: Managing Partner, BrainBox Consulting
value.source: SF OpportunityContactRole (Evaluator), partner contact, email jai@brainbox.consulting, SF Next_Step__c 2 Jun 2026
value.sentiment: Neutral to positive (positioning Zycus in vendor scan per Next Step, but no execution evidence)

value.name: SAP Ariba
value.quote: ERP migration timing gives the window to align the Ariba replacement
value.how_we_win: Ariba is being replaced, so threat is low unless they re-engage with a retention offer. Win by positioning Merlin AI (negotiation, contracting, intake agents) and full S2P stack integration as the modern, AI-native alternative Ariba cannot match, especially if buyer priorities include procurement automation, supplier collaboration, and AI-driven savings. Must first confirm why Ariba is out (cost, fit, lack of innovation) and whether they are still in the running or fully eliminated.

value.detail: No Economic Buyer or Decision Maker identified. No executive-level engagement. Single point of contact (Director-level procurement, Kristin Boehme) via third-party consultant. No visibility into CFO, finance, or C-suite sponsorship for a $250k, 9-module S2P stack. EB mapping is a condition for advancing to the relevant stakeholder or Negotiation.
value.source: SF OpportunityContactRole 26 Jun 2026, SF Q3 MEDDPICC fields empty, zero buyer calls

packets.subject: Zero buyer engagement 50 days post-creation. No discovery, no demo, no MEDDPICC coverage, no competitive intel. Deal may be a placeholder or already lost in a vendor scan Zycus is not actively participating in. Dark deal risk is critical.

value.flag: RE-ENGAGE IMMEDIATELY via BrainBox and Kristin Boehme. Deal is 50 days old with zero buyer interaction. The Next Step (updated 2 Jun, 24 days ago) describes a plan (BrainBox scouting, demo to be arranged, pricing to be finalized) but shows no execution. Without a discovery call or demo in the next 7 days, Zycus risks being dropped from the shortlist or never making it onto the list. The single highest-leverage move is for Caroline Lacocque (deal owner) to contact Jai Bharat Maram (BrainBox) and Kristin Boehme directly this week to confirm Zycus is still in the vendor scan, schedule the discovery/demo call, and surface the competitive shortlist, buyer priorities, and evaluation timeline. If no reply in 10 business days, escalate to John Woodcock (owner's manager) for exec-to-exec reach-out to Centrient procurement leadership (Nikolaas Rossillion, Global Head of Supply Chain, Procurement, if confirmed as stakeholder).

value.flag: MULTI-THREAD beyond Kristin Boehme and establish a direct Zycus-Centrient relationship. Deal is single-threaded to one buyer contact (Kristin Boehme, Director Global Procurement) via a third-party consultant (BrainBox). No contact with Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, supply chain head, IT, or finance. Single point of contact creates ghost risk and blocks MEDDPICC discovery. During the first call with Kristin/BrainBox, ask for introductions to the broader buying committee: the EB (likely CFO or finance director), the supply chain or procurement head (possibly Nikolaas Rossillion), IT lead (Philippe Caby, CIO, on account contact list), and any other evaluation stakeholders. Add confirmed stakeholders as OpportunityContactRoles. Multi-threading is a condition for advancing past Qualified stage.

No AIS score, no AIS status, no AI appetite signal (SF Q2 AIS fields empty).

BrainBox role and commercial structure ambiguous (advisory vs SI, direct contract vs partner-channeled).
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: 'Vendor Selected')
